segments.text: Then Balaam said to Balak,
Build seven altars for me here, and prepare for me here seven bulls and seven rams.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: And Balak did just as Balaam had spoken, and Balak and Balaam offered a bull and a ram on each altar.
Then Balaam said to Balak,
Stand by your burnt offering, and I will go; perhaps the Lord will come to meet me, and whatever He shows me I will tell you.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: So he went to a desolate height.
And God met Balaam, and he said to Him,
I have prepared the seven altars, and I have offered on each altar a bull and a ram.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Then the Lord put a word in Balaam's mouth, and said,
Return to Balak, and thus you shall speak.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: So he returned to him, and there he was, standing by his burnt offering, he and all the princes of Moab.
And he took up his oracle and said:
Balak the king of Moab has brought me from Aram, From the mountains of the east. 'Come, curse Jacob for me, And come, denounce Israel!'
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: How shall I curse whom God has not cursed? And how shall I denounce whom the Lord has not denounced?
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: For from the top of the rocks I see him, And from the hills I behold him; There! A people dwelling alone, Not reckoning itself among the nations.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Who can count the dust of Jacob, Or number one-fourth of Israel? Let me die the death of the righteous, And let my end be like his!
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Then Balak said to Balaam,
What have you done to me? I took you to curse my enemies, and look, you have blessed them bountifully!
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: So he answered and said,
Must I not take heed to speak what the Lord has put in my mouth?
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Then Balak said to him,
Please come with me to another place from which you may see them; you shall see only the outer part of them, and shall not see them all; curse them for me from there.
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: So he brought him to the field of Zophim, to the top of Pisgah, and built seven altars, and offered a bull and a ram on each altar.
And he said to Balak,
Stand here by your burnt offering while I meet the Lord over there.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Then the Lord met Balaam, and put a word in his mouth, and said,
Go back to Balak, and thus you shall speak.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: So he came to him, and there he was, standing by his burnt offering, and the princes of Moab were with him. And Balak said to him,
What has the Lord spoken?
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: Then he took up his oracle and said:
Rise up, Balak, and hear! Listen to me, son of Zippor!
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: God is not a man, that He should lie, Nor a son of man, that He should repent. Has He said, and will He not do? Or has He spoken, and will He not make it good?
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Behold, I have received a command to bless; He has blessed, and I cannot reverse it.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: He has not observed iniquity in Jacob, Nor has He seen wickedness in Israel. The Lord his God is with him, And the shout of a King is among them.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: God brings them out of Egypt; He has strength like a wild ox.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: For there is no sorcery against Jacob, Nor any divination against Israel. It now must be said of Jacob And of Israel, 'Oh, what God has done!'
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Look, a people rises like a lioness, And lifts itself up like a lion; It shall not lie down until it devours the prey, And drinks the blood of the slain.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Then Balak said to Balaam,
Neither curse them at all, nor bless them at all!
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: So Balaam answered and said to Balak,
Did I not tell you, saying, 'All that the Lord speaks, that I must do'?
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Then Balak said to Balaam,
Please come, I will take you to another place; perhaps it will please God that you may curse them for me from there.
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: So Balak took Balaam to the top of Peor, that overlooks the wasteland.
Then Balaam said to Balak,
Build for me here seven altars, and prepare for me here seven bulls and seven rams.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: And Balak did as Balaam had said, and offered a bull and a ram on every altar.
